text:
Background:
A mid-sized hospital group currently manages outpatient appointments through phone calls and spreadsheets. Patients often wait on hold for a long time, staff manually re-enter details into multiple systems, and appointment slots are sometimes double-booked or left unused because cancellations are not surfaced quickly.

Actors:
Patients
Reception staff
Clinic managers
Doctors

Problem:
The hospital needs a digital appointment scheduling system that reduces call volume, makes it easier for patients to book or reschedule appointments, and gives staff better visibility into doctor availability.

Goals:
Allow patients to book, reschedule, and cancel appointments online.
Reduce manual work for reception staff.
Improve appointment utilization by filling cancelled slots faster.
Provide clinic managers with visibility into no-shows, cancellations, and schedule efficiency.

Features:
Patients should be able to search by specialty, location, and earliest available appointment.
Patients should receive confirmation and reminder notifications by SMS or email.
Reception staff should be able to create bookings on behalf of patients.
The system should prevent double-booking of doctors and rooms.
Clinic managers should have a dashboard showing cancellations, no-shows, and utilization trends.
High-priority patients should be flagged for faster scheduling when appropriate.

Constraints:
The first release should support web only, not mobile apps.
The system must comply with healthcare privacy requirements.
It must integrate with the hospital's existing patient record system.
The product should be usable by non-technical administrative staff with minimal training.
Reminder notifications should be configurable per clinic.
